name: analytics
description: Server-side behavioral event fan-out to product analytics platforms. Answers 'how are users behaving in the product?'. Fires FINE-GRAINED product-usage events (feature_viewed, action_completed, funnel_step_reached, experiment_variant_assigned, screen_time_recorded, cta_clicked, form_submitted, error_encountered, ...) that complement the FRONTEND `@academorix/analytics` package's client-side capture. Server-side is the AUTHORITATIVE source for events that MUST NOT be lost (payment succeeded, mutation completed, backend-driven state transitions) which browser-side capture drops to ad-blockers and iOS ITP. Ships FOUR tables: `analytics_events` (canonical ledger, 2-year retention), `analytics_deliveries` (per-provider log, 90-day retention), `analytics_provider_configs` (per-tenant provider credentials + enabled_event_types + sampling_rate + circuit-breaker), `analytics_identities` (identify → user_id resolution table — maps anonymous_id → identified_user_id across sessions). MultipleInstanceManager via `AnalyticsProviderManager` with 7 driver day-1: PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment (fan-out to N destinations behind Segment), June, Google Analytics 4 (yes, GA4 appears in both analytics and marketing — different event categories, same wire protocol), Custom Webhook (generic escape hatch). Each provider drives its own event batching (Segment batches; Mixpanel batches; PostHog can batch or single). Consent-gated under `analytics` consent category (distinct from `marketing`). Attribution snapshot pulled at event-create time. Server-side identify + track + page + screen + alias + group verbs. Sampling per-event-type + per-provider — PostHog might get 100%, Amplitude might get 10% for volume-heavy event types.
planned_consumers: marketing (Wave 5 — marketing conversion events optionally cross-post to analytics for funnel unification)
finance (Wave 5 — subscription lifecycle events emit fine-grained analytics per state transition)
referrals (Wave 5 — referral funnel steps fire as analytics events: link_clicked, referee_registered, referee_converted)
notifications (Wave 5 — notification open/click/dismiss surface as analytics for per-persona optimisation)

attribute_driven.description: Modules opt in to server-side analytics capture via attributes. Discovery pass at boot compiles the AnalyticsEventCapturerRegistry so runtime dispatch is a hash-map lookup, never reflection. Provider drivers register via a separate attribute so custom drivers can be shipped by third-party modules.

attributes.AsAnalyticsEventCapturer: Marks a listener class as an analytics event capturer. `#[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer(domainEvent: OrderPlaced::class, analyticsEventName: 'Order Completed', category: 'funnel_step')]`. The capturer receives a domain event and produces one (or more) analytics events. Many-to-one: a single domain event can map to multiple analytics events with different property shapes.
attributes.AsAnalyticsProviderDriver: Registers a custom AnalyticsProviderDriver at boot. `#[AsAnalyticsProviderDriver(name: 'my_custom_analytics', supportsBatching: true, maxBatchSize: 500)]`. Enables third-party modules to add drivers without editing the manager.

provider_matrix.description: Seven drivers day-1 covering the mainstream product-analytics landscape. Each driver ships with a payload transformer + a batching policy + a retry policy + a signature-verification strategy for provider webhooks (bounce / retry hints).

drivers.name: posthog
drivers.endpoint: https://{host}/capture/ (default host: app.posthog.com; EU host: eu.posthog.com)
drivers.payload_shape: { api_key, event, distinct_id, properties: { ...event_properties, $set: user_properties }, timestamp }
drivers.session_recording: opt-in via config.enable_session_recording; feature-gated by `analytics_session_recording` entitlement

drivers.name: amplitude
drivers.payload_shape: { api_key, events: [{ event_type, user_id, device_id, event_properties, user_properties, session_id, time, ip, platform, os_name, os_version }] }

drivers.name: segment
drivers.note: Segment fans out to N downstream destinations configured tenant-side in Segment console. Academorix sees Segment as one provider; effective destinations are Segment's problem.

drivers.name: june
drivers.note: B2B-analytics oriented. No page/screen verbs — mapped to track.

drivers.name: google_analytics_4
drivers.payload_shape: { client_id, user_id, timestamp_micros, events: [{ name, params }] }
drivers.note: SAME wire protocol as marketing::ga4 driver but DIFFERENT event_name catalog. Analytics events use snake_case behavioral names (screen_view, select_content, view_item); marketing events use business names (sign_up, purchase, generate_lead). Distinct tenant configs allowed — typically the same GA4 property is used for both, filtered client-side in GA4 by event_name.

drivers.name: custom_webhook
drivers.note: Generic escape hatch. Sensitive escape hatch — endpoint URL locked to https:// + validated against tenant's allowed_egress_domains before dispatch.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing analytics reads (event log, delivery log, provider CRUD, identity resolution, funnel + experiment reports) + admin merge/deactivate actions. Every route requires the `analytics_capture` entitlement; merge routes additionally require `analytics_identity_merge` (Enterprise); advanced providers require `analytics_advanced_providers` (Medium+); sampling configuration routes require `analytics_sampling_config` (Enterprise); funnel + experiment routes require `analytics_extended_retention` (Enterprise for beyond 2y).

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Delivery inspection + circuit-breaker dashboard for on-call.
